bead.title: Classify repeated pre-claim system warnings as operator-visible worker s: part 2
bead.description:
PROBLEM
Parent bead ddx-eea2dfca is too broad for one execution pass. This child owns part 2 of 3 of that parent scope.

ROOT CAUSE
PROBLEM
A Niflheim worker repeatedly emitted pre-claim readiness/intake warnings for the same systemic dirty/generated DDx artifact condition and continued polling/releasing. That looks like forward progress but does not create a clear operator-visible system-unready lane after the configured threshold.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1967-2072` emits pre-claim timeout/system-unready outcomes as warn-only by default and continues implementation when strict intake blocking is off.
- `cli/internal/agent/failclass/failclass.go:103-142` says setup/dirty worktree/system outcomes should not escalate, but repeated pre-claim warnings are not consistently classified into retry/operator-attention decisions before worker retry loops continue.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a self-contained Niflheim-style pre-claim fixture or table-driven case under `cli/internal/agent/...` for repeated readiness timeout/system-unready warnings with generated DDx artifact dirt such as `.ddx/harness-sessions/...json`. After the configured same-fingerprint threshold, classify the condition as system-unready/operator-attention and release/park without implementation retry or model escalation. Align with `ddx-d67f124e` rather than duplicating its lower-level implementation.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Fizeau route selection or hard-code concrete provider/model names.
- Do not edit Niflheim beads or Niflheim implementation code.
- Do not duplicate the low-level index-contention fix tracked by `ddx-2965925e`.
- Do not duplicate the post-commit review/land timeout work tracked by `ddx-b2846610`.
- Do not duplicate the repeated pre-claim warn operator-attention bead `ddx-d67f124e`; this bead only adds the Niflheim observation as regression coverage and decision/audit wiring.
- Do not implement a full operator-lane product workflow beyond state transitions/events needed to keep the worker from blind retry or false close.

INTERSECTIONS
Parent: `ddx-3106674f`. Dependencies to preserve: `ddx-2965925e`, `ddx-b2846610`, `ddx-d67f124e`. Related: `ddx-e1a576a7`, `ddx-a9d130d0`, `ddx-ac10e7af`. Governing references: FEAT-010, TP-021, ADR-024, docs/dev/engineering-principles.md:39-45.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement only this child acceptance slice:
- `TestExecuteBeadResult_NiflheimEvidence_PreClaimDecisionAudit` proves result JSON/event bodies include `failure_class`, `retry_action`, `escalation_count`, requested/actual routing facts, pre-claim fingerprint/threshold facts, and operator-attention reason for the repeated warning family.
- Existing related behavior from `ddx-d67f124e` remains covered by reuse/alignment with its helpers or by waiting on that dependency; this bead does not add a duplicate pre-claim warning escalation implementation.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement parent acceptance criteria assigned to sibling child beads. Preserve every parent non-scope constraint unless this child explicitly narrows it.
bead.acceptance:
1. `TestExecuteBeadResult_NiflheimEvidence_PreClaimDecisionAudit` proves result JSON/event bodies include `failure_class`, `retry_action`, `escalation_count`, requested/actual routing facts, pre-claim fingerprint/threshold facts, and operator-attention reason for the repeated warning family.
2. Existing related behavior from `ddx-d67f124e` remains covered by reuse/alignment with its helpers or by waiting on that dependency; this bead does not add a duplicate pre-claim warning escalation implementation.
